title: Bitcoin Clears $71K as Spot ETF Inflows Hit $2.1B in Six Days
subtitle: Six consecutive sessions of positive ETF flows put the quarter back in the green
metaDescription: Bitcoin breaks $71K on sustained spot ETF inflows totaling $2.1 billion over six trading sessions.
heroImageAlt: Market trading floor in warm amber light
bodyHtml:
Bitcoin cleared $71,000 on Tuesday, extending a move that began when spot ETF flows turned positive for a sixth consecutive session — the longest streak of the quarter. Net inflows totaled $2.1 billion over the run.

net ETF inflows, six sessions

Derivatives desks read the move as positioning rather than conviction. Funding rates are flat, open interest is climbing slowly, and options pricing implies the calmest month since January.
pullQuote: Calm, in this market, is usually the part to distrust.

title: Ethereum Staking Yield Drops to 3.1% as Validator Queue Lengthens
subtitle: Record validator count compresses annualized returns, but liquid staking protocols absorb the gap
metaDescription: Ethereum staking yield falls to 3.1% as validator queue reaches new highs.
heroImageAlt: Abstract network visualization in blue tones
bodyHtml:
Ethereum's annualized staking yield has compressed to 3.1%, the lowest since the Merge, as the validator queue reached a record 1.04 million active validators. More capital chasing the same block rewards mechanically shrinks per-validator returns.

Liquid staking protocols are absorbing the difference: Lido and Rocket Pool saw combined deposits of $840 million in May, as retail holders prioritized liquidity over yield optimization.
pullQuote: More validators, same rewards. The math is simple.

title: Solana DEX Volume Surpasses Ethereum for Third Consecutive Month
subtitle: Meme coin activity and lower fees keep Solana's decentralized exchanges ahead of the incumbent
metaDescription: Solana decentralized exchanges outpace Ethereum by volume for a third month running.
heroImageAlt: Blockchain network nodes illustrated in green
bodyHtml:
Decentralized exchange volume on Solana topped $48 billion in May, ahead of Ethereum's $41 billion for a third consecutive month. Meme coin trading drove roughly 35% of Solana's figure, while Ethereum's volume concentrated in stablecoin pairs and blue-chip DeFi protocols.

Solana DEX volume, May 2026

Fee revenue per transaction remains about 60x lower on Solana, sustaining its cost advantage for high-frequency retail traders.
pullQuote: Three months is a trend. Six months is a regime change.

title: Fed Holds Rates; Powell Signals Two Cuts Remain Possible in 2026
subtitle: FOMC minutes show broad consensus for patience, but the door to cuts stays open if inflation cooperates
metaDescription: Federal Reserve holds rates steady with Powell keeping two 2026 cuts on the table.
bodyHtml:
The Federal Reserve held the federal funds rate at 4.25–4.50% on Wednesday, with Chair Powell stating that two additional cuts remain "plausible" in 2026 if core PCE continues its gradual descent. The decision was unanimous.

Markets had priced roughly 1.8 cuts heading into the meeting. The unchanged outcome moved Treasury yields fractionally lower on the two-year and left risk assets broadly flat.
pullQuote: Patience is a policy, not a pause.

title: BlackRock Crypto AUM Crosses $30B as Institutional Demand Accelerates
subtitle: The asset manager's digital asset products now represent its fastest-growing category by inflows
metaDescription: BlackRock crypto assets under management hit $30 billion driven by ETF inflows.
bodyHtml:
BlackRock's cryptocurrency assets under management crossed $30 billion on Monday, driven primarily by inflows into its IBIT Bitcoin ETF and a newer Ether product launched in March. The figure represents a 140% increase from year-end 2025.

BlackRock crypto AUM

The growth rate makes digital assets BlackRock's fastest-growing category by net inflows, ahead of emerging-market equity and sustainable bond funds.

title: Tether Mints $3B USDT in 48 Hours, Largest Single-Week Issuance
subtitle: On-chain data shows the stablecoin supply surge preceding recent spot market moves
metaDescription: Tether issues $3 billion USDT in 48 hours, the largest single-week issuance on record.
heroImageAlt: Digital currency symbols on dark background
bodyHtml:
Tether minted $3 billion in USDT over a 48-hour window ending Tuesday — the largest single-week issuance since the stablecoin's inception. On-chain analysts note the timing preceded Bitcoin's push above $71,000 by roughly 18 hours, a pattern consistent with prior bull-leg setups.

USDT's circulating supply now sits at $118 billion, widening its lead over USDC, which has grown more slowly under stricter compliance requirements.

title: MicroStrategy Converts to Bitcoin Treasury Corp, Renames to Strategy
subtitle: The company's entire balance sheet is now denominated in BTC; equity serves as leveraged exposure
metaDescription: MicroStrategy rebrands to Strategy and formalizes its identity as a Bitcoin treasury corporation.
heroImageAlt: Corporate office building with glass facade
bodyHtml:
Strategy Inc., formerly MicroStrategy, completed its formal conversion to a Bitcoin treasury corporation on Friday, filing an amended charter that codifies BTC holdings as the sole treasury reserve asset. The company holds 214,400 BTC at an average cost basis of $35,100.

The move formalized what has been operational reality for three years. Equity analysts who cover the stock treat it as a BTC proxy with a management fee embedded in the premium to net asset value.

title: Coinbase Futures Volume Hits All-Time High as Retail Returns
subtitle: Open interest in perpetual contracts jumped 44% last week on the regulated exchange
metaDescription: Coinbase records all-time high futures volume as retail traders return to the market.
heroImageAlt: Exchange trading dashboard with candlestick charts
bodyHtml:
Coinbase reported record futures trading volume last week, with open interest in perpetual contracts climbing 44% to $9.2 billion. The exchange attributed the move to returning retail participants drawn by the ETF-driven price action.

perpetual OI, week-over-week

Institutional flow, measured by block trade share, actually declined as a percentage of total volume — a sign that the marginal buyer is again the small account rather than the portfolio manager.

title: Grayscale Converts GBTC Discount to Premium for First Time Since 2021
subtitle: Shares trade above net asset value as redemption pressure reverses into fresh demand
metaDescription: GBTC trades at a premium to NAV for the first time since 2021.
heroImageAlt: Investment fund office with screens showing market data
bodyHtml:
Grayscale Bitcoin Trust shares closed Tuesday at a 0.3% premium to net asset value, the first positive reading since the product converted to an ETF structure in January 2024. The trust had traded at discounts exceeding 40% for much of 2022 and 2023.

Analysts attributed the flip to exhaustion of forced selling from creditors and bankruptcy estates that dominated early redemptions after the ETF conversion.

title: Ripple Wins SEC Case; XRP Relisted on Three Major US Exchanges
subtitle: The district court ruling ends four years of regulatory overhang and opens the door to institutional custody
metaDescription: Ripple wins SEC lawsuit; XRP immediately relisted on major US cryptocurrency exchanges.
bodyHtml:
A federal district court ruled in Ripple's favor on all remaining counts in its four-year lawsuit with the SEC, finding that XRP token sales to retail buyers did not constitute unregistered securities offerings. The ruling is the most significant crypto regulatory decision since the Howey framework was applied to digital assets in 2017.

Coinbase, Kraken, and Gemini relisted XRP within six hours of the ruling. The token jumped 38% on the news before retracing to a 22% gain by close.
